opp.name: NYCreates_iSupplier+iRisk+eProc_Opp_June'26
opp.next_step: JRA 7/1: Crowdsourcing feedback from Joe on demo videos.
opp.ais_why:
• NY CREATES runs a 'currently reactive' centralized procurement 'with limited visibility'; the driver is basic process and visibility, not AI.
• Only Merlin is scoped and the Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set.
• The next step is 'crowdsourcing feedback... on demo videos,' an early, low-intent activity with no AI enthusiasm captured.

items.quote: Intro call 15 Jun with Joe Schell completed but no recording was captured (bot denied, not recorded, or cancelled post-facto). This is the only buyer touchpoint in 16 days, and we have no buyer-stated pain, metrics, decision criteria, or competitive intelligence from it.

Discovery call promised around 21 Jun has not been scheduled as of 01 Jul. Without it, all MEDDPICC elements (pain, metrics, EB, criteria, process, champion) remain gaps, and we cannot validate fit, build urgency, or position against JAGGAER.
items.source: Living memory: discovery promised ~21 Jun; Next_Step 01 Jul shows no scheduled call

items.quote: Only 1 buyer-side contact role on this opportunity (Joe Schell, Assistant VP Procurement). CFO Nathan Bull, CIO Maurice Raghubar, COO Paul Kelly, and other C-level executives at the account are not engaged. At $125k and a March 2027 close, power-role access (CFO or COO) is required to de-risk budget authority and approval chain.
items.source: Salesforce contact roles: 1 buyer contact; account contacts show CFO, CIO, COO not engaged on opp

sources: Avoma manifest: 1 touchpoint (15 Jun intro), no recording
Next_Step 01 Jul: awaiting feedback on demo videos, discovery not scheduled
metrics.narrative: No business problem or quantified impact has been discussed. The intro call on 15 Jun had no recording, so we have no buyer-stated pain, cost savings target, cycle-time reduction, or ROI expectation. The discovery call (promised around 21 Jun, not yet scheduled) is the gate to uncovering this. Without metrics, we cannot build a defensible business case or justify the $125k investment against JAGGAER. The move: schedule discovery this week and extract the procurement pain (manual processes, compliance gaps, supplier risk, spend visibility) and any quantified targets (headcount, savings %, error rate reduction). Source: Avoma manifest shows no recorded calls with content; Next_Step 01 Jul indicates rep is awaiting feedback but discovery has not happened.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: Joe Schell (Decision Maker), no Champion role
Avoma manifest: 1 touchpoint (15 Jun), no recording, no follow-up
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. Joe Schell (Assistant VP, Procurement, Decision Maker per contact role) is the sole contact, but we have no evidence of advocacy, internal navigation, or power-role access. The intro call (no recording) and the 16 days since have produced no champion-building activity (no shared docs, no introductions to CFO/CIO/COO, no pushing our agenda internally). The move: assess Schell's willingness to champion us during discovery, and if he is passive or gatekeeping, multi-thread to the CFO or COO to find a power sponsor. Source: Salesforce contact roles show Joe Schell only; Avoma manifest shows one touchpoint with no follow-up.

sources: Avoma manifest: no recorded calls with competitive intelligence
competition.narrative: JAGGAER is the named competitor (source: Salesforce Competitors__c). We have zero intelligence on their position: are they the incumbent, a peer in the RFP, or preferred? What is their pricing, their strength in iSupplier/eProcurement/iRisk vs ours, and how is NY Creates comparing us? The intro call (no recording) did not surface this, and the discovery call has not happened. The move: use discovery to extract JAGGAER's role, their perceived strengths and weaknesses, and how the buyer is evaluating us against them. Our wedge (once we know their position): Merlin AI-driven sourcing and negotiation, deeper iRisk integration, and faster implementation vs JAGGAER's legacy complexity. Source: Salesforce Competitors__c = JAGGAER; no other competitive intelligence.

sources: Salesforce: no Customer_Business_Problem__c or pain fields
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. We do not know what is broken in their current procurement stack, why they are evaluating new vendors, what the cost of inaction is, or who owns the pain. The intro call on 15 Jun (no recording) did not capture this. Without pain, we cannot build urgency or justify the investment. The move: use discovery to extract the specific pain points (manual PO processes, supplier risk gaps, compliance failures, lack of spend visibility, no AI-driven sourcing) and name the pain owner (Joe Schell, CFO, or COO). Source: Avoma manifest shows no recorded calls; Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c and related fields are blank.

sources: Avoma manifest: no recorded calls with content
paper_process.narrative: No contracting or procurement mechanics have been discussed. We do not know if NY Creates requires an MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or ERP integration scoping (given iSupplier and eProcurement are in scope). We do not know if they work with an SI or partner, or what their procurement channel is. The intro call (no recording) did not cover this, and the discovery call has not happened. The move: ask during discovery what their contracting process looks like, who leads legal/InfoSec reviews, and what integrations they require. Source: Avoma manifest shows no recorded calls; Salesforce has no contracting or paper-process fields populated.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: Joe Schell (Decision Maker), no EB or Exec Sponsor
Account contacts: Nathan Bull (CFO), Paul Kelly (COO) not engaged
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is mapped. Joe Schell (Assistant VP, Procurement) is the sole contact and holds the Decision Maker role, but at $125k and a March 2027 close, budget authority likely sits with the relevant stakeholder or COO Paul Kelly. Neither has been engaged. The intro call (no recording) did not surface the approval chain or budget owner. Without EB access, we cannot validate budget availability, secure commercial sign-off, or de-risk the forecast. The move: use discovery to map the approval process and multi-thread into the CFO or COO within 14 days. Source: Salesforce contact roles show Joe Schell only; account contacts include Nathan Bull (CFO) and Paul Kelly (COO), neither engaged on this opp.

sources: Salesforce: no What_is_the_decision_process__c or related fields
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unmapped. We do not know if NY Creates is running a formal RFP, how many vendors are being evaluated, who the evaluation committee includes, what the approval chain looks like (CFO, COO, board), or what gates exist (legal, InfoSec, procurement, contracting). The intro call (no recording) did not surface this. The March 2027 close date is placeholder-quality with no process milestones to anchor it. The move: use discovery to map the buying committee, the approval chain, and the timeline (RFI, demos, shortlist, commercials, contract). Source: Avoma manifest shows no recorded calls; Salesforce has no process documentation.

sources: Avoma manifest: no recorded calls with content
Salesforce: no Business_Requirements__c or decision-criteria fields populated
decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria have been documented. We do not know what NY Creates is optimizing for (cost, compliance, supplier risk management, ERP integration, AI/Merlin capabilities, ease of use), how they weight iSupplier vs eProcurement vs iRisk vs Merlin in their decision, or what JAGGAER is being measured against. The intro call (no recording) did not capture this, and the discovery call has not happened. Without criteria, we cannot tailor our positioning or validate product-market fit. The move: use discovery to extract their RFP/RFI requirements, pain priorities, and how they will score vendors. Source: Avoma manifest shows no recorded calls; Next_Step 01 Jul indicates demo-video feedback but no discovery.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 20 is carried by differentiation strength +1.00 (weight 20) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'consolidat' in…; business case strength +1.00 (weight 10) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'savings' in…. Offsetting it: momentum 44 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 44 (slipping backward): buyer has gone quiet — no buyer-side activity in the last 30 days. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 5 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 44 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 4 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 12 is anchored on the stage win (20), nudged by commitment 44 and momentum 44, and dragged by risk 14.

contributions.evidence: differentiation strength +1.00 (weight 20) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'consolidat' in Next-Step/narrative

items.name: Joe Schell
items.risk: Single point of contact with zero follow-up engagement. Schell has not responded to rep's promised Showpad link or discovery-call scheduling (both committed ~21 Jun, neither actioned). At Assistant VP level, likely lacks autonomy to approve $125k budget without CFO/COO sign-off. No evidence of advocacy, urgency, or internal sponsorship. Risk: passive evaluator or misaligned timeline.

items.name: Maurice Raghubar

items.detail: Single-threaded into Joe Schell (Assistant VP, Procurement) with no CFO, CIO, or COO engagement. At $125k and a March 2027 close, we need power-role sponsorship (CFO Nathan Bull or COO Paul Kelly) to de-risk budget authority and approval chain. Schell may not have autonomy to commit without C-level sign-off.

Deal is 11 days old with one intro call (no recording) and zero follow-up engagement. Rep promised Showpad link and discovery scheduling on ~21 Jun but has not actioned it. With 264 days to close, there is runway, but a deal that goes dark from day one signals low buyer urgency or that we are not yet relevant to their timeline.

No pain, no metrics, no decision criteria, no competitive intelligence, no Economic Buyer mapped. Every MEDDPICC element is a gap. The discovery call (promised but not scheduled) is the gate to everything; without it, we cannot build a defensible case, position against JAGGAER, or multi-thread into power roles.

Deal is 13 days old and dark from inception (zero buyer engagement, no LastActivityDate, no Avoma calls). Rep promised Showpad link and discovery call ~21 Jun but has not actioned either. With 262 days to close, there is runway, but a deal that goes silent on day one signals low buyer urgency or that we are not yet relevant to their procurement timeline. Risk: NY Creates is slow-playing vendors, JAGGAER is further ahead, or Schell is not the real driver.

MEDDPICC entirely unmapped (no pain, no metrics, no criteria, no process, no EB, no champion). Discovery call (not yet scheduled) is the gate to everything. All MEDDPICC elements are gaps (no pain, no metrics, no decision criteria, no Economic Buyer, no champion, no process mapped). The intro call (no recording) and the missing discovery call mean we are flying blind. Without MEDDPICC, we cannot build a defensible case, validate fit or urgency, position against JAGGAER, or forecast with confidence. The discovery call is the gate to everything; it is 10 days overdue (promised ~21 Jun).

JAGGAER is named as competitor but we have zero competitive intelligence (their position, pricing, product overlap, buyer preference). The intro call (no recording) did not surface this, and the discovery call has not happened. Without intel, we cannot differentiate, position our Merlin AI wedge, or defend against their strengths. Risk: JAGGAER is further ahead and we are blind to it.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 15 Jun 2026 (16 days ago), close date 17 Mar 2027 (259 days out). Time in Qualified: 16 days. No LastActivityDate on record. One buyer touchpoint: intro call 15 Jun with Joe Schell (no recording). Next_Step dated 01 Jul shows rep reaching out for feedback on demo videos, but this is rep-initiated outreach, not buyer engagement. The discovery call promised around 21 Jun has not happened. No forward movement in 16 days. Pace required: discovery, multi-thread to CFO/COO, MEDDPICC build, competitive positioning, and commercials across 259 days is achievable, but only if the buyer re-engages this week. Slowing because the key action (schedule discovery) is stalled and buyer engagement has been silent since day one.
evidence: Pipeline forecast is indefensible: no Economic Buyer mapped, no champion, no pain or metrics documented, single-threaded into a mid-level procurement contact, and zero buyer engagement since the 15 Jun intro call. A $125k deal forecast for Mar 2027 requires power-role sponsorship, a validated business case, and forward momentum. None exist. Recommended forecast: Omitted until discovery completes and EB/CFO access is secured (source: Salesforce contact roles, Next_Step 01 Jul, Avoma manifest).
north_star_verdict.headline: 16-day-old deal with one intro call (no recording) and zero follow-up buyer engagement. Rep has reached out today (01 Jul) awaiting feedback on demo videos, but the discovery call promised ~21 Jun has not been scheduled. With JAGGAER already in the mix and no pain, metrics, or power-role access mapped, the deal is stalled at the gate.

flags: SINGLE-THREADED AND NO CHAMPION — Joe Schell (Assistant VP, Procurement) is the only buyer-side contact role, and with no engagement since the intro call 11 days ago, he is showing no champion behavior (no multi-threading, no urgency, no advocacy). At a $125k deal with a March 2027 close, we need at least 3-4 power contacts (CFO, CIO/IT Ops, COO) and a champion who is actively driving the evaluation internally. The discovery call is the gate to ask Schell to introduce us to the CFO (Nathan Bull) and CIO (Maurice Raghubar) so we can map the decision process and budget authority. If Schell is unwilling to multi-thread us, he is not a champion — he is a polite responder — and we need to find another entry point or disqualify.
best_practice_check.summary: 16-day-old deal with zero discovery, single-threaded into mid-level procurement, and JAGGAER in the mix with no competitive intelligence. The highest-impact lever: schedule discovery this week to unlock MEDDPICC, multi-thread to CFO/COO, and position against JAGGAER before they lock in.

The move: use discovery to gauge AI appetite (are they AI Hungry, Curious, or Resistant), position Merlin as the differentiation vs JAGGAER, and anchor it to their procurement pain (manual sourcing, supplier negotiation, spend optimization).

hard.ais_why:
• NY CREATES runs a 'currently reactive' centralized procurement 'with limited visibility'; the driver is basic process and visibility, not AI.
• Only Merlin is scoped and the Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set.
• The next step is 'crowdsourcing feedback... on demo videos,' an early, low-intent activity with no AI enthusiasm captured.

deltas.to: Deal is 16 days old with one intro call (no recording) and zero follow-up engagement. Rep reached out today (01 Jul) awaiting feedback on demo videos, but disco
deltas.subject: Deal is 16 days old with one intro call (no recording) and zero follow-up engagement. Rep reached out today (01 Jul) awaiting feedback on demo videos, but discovery call promised ~21 Jun has not been scheduled. With 259 days to close, there is runway, but a deal that goes dark from day one signals low buyer urgency or that we are not yet relevant to their procurement timeline. Risk: NY Creates is slow-playing vendors, JAGGAER is further ahead, or Schell is not the real driver.

deltas.to: All MEDDPICC elements are gaps (no pain, no metrics, no decision criteria, no Economic Buyer, no champion, no process mapped). The intro call (no recording) and
deltas.subject: All MEDDPICC elements are gaps (no pain, no metrics, no decision criteria, no Economic Buyer, no champion, no process mapped). The intro call (no recording) and the missing discovery call mean we are flying blind. Without MEDDPICC, we cannot build a defensible case, validate fit or urgency, position against JAGGAER, or forecast with confidence. The discovery call is the gate to everything; it is 10 days overdue (promised ~21 Jun).

deltas.to: JAGGAER is named as competitor but we have zero competitive intelligence (their position, pricing, product overlap, buyer preference). The intro call (no record
deltas.subject: JAGGAER is named as competitor but we have zero competitive intelligence (their position, pricing, product overlap, buyer preference). The intro call (no recording) did not surface this, and the discovery call has not happened. Without intel, we cannot differentiate, position our Merlin AI wedge, or defend against their strengths. Risk: JAGGAER is further ahead and we are blind to it.

deltas.to: Schedule discovery call with Joe Schell this week (by 04 Jul) to extract pain, metrics, decision criteria, Economic Buyer identity, approval process, and JAGGAE
deltas.subject: Schedule discovery call with Joe Schell this week (by 04 Jul) to extract pain, metrics, decision criteria, Economic Buyer identity, approval process, and JAGGAER's position. This is the single gate to unlocking the deal: without it, all MEDDPICC elements stay dark, we cannot validate fit or urgency, and we cannot build a defensible forecast. Discovery is 10 days overdue (promised ~21 Jun); do not let it drift to next week.

deltas.to: Multi-thread into power roles (CFO Nathan Bull or COO Paul Kelly) within 14 days to de-risk budget authority and approval chain. At $125k and a March 2027 close
deltas.subject: Multi-thread into power roles (CFO Nathan Bull or COO Paul Kelly) within 14 days to de-risk budget authority and approval chain. At $125k and a March 2027 close, Joe Schell (Assistant VP, Procurement) likely cannot commit without C-level sign-off. Use discovery to map the buying committee and secure an introduction to the CFO or COO. Single-threading into a mid-level procurement contact on a six-figure deal is a forecast killer.

deltas.to: Extract JAGGAER competitive intelligence during discovery: are they the incumbent, a peer finalist, or preferred? What is their pricing, product overlap, and pe
deltas.subject: Extract JAGGAER competitive intelligence during discovery: are they the incumbent, a peer finalist, or preferred? What is their pricing, product overlap, and perceived strengths vs our iSupplier/eProcurement/Merlin/iRisk scope? With zero intel, we cannot differentiate or position our AI/Merlin wedge. Use discovery to surface their weaknesses and build our how-we-win narrative before they lock in.

deltas.to: COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE MISSING: JAGGAER named as competitor but zero intel on incumbent vs parallel eval, their scope, pricing, or buyer sentiment. No positio
deltas.subject: COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE MISSING: JAGGAER named as competitor but zero intel on incumbent vs parallel eval, their scope, pricing, or buyer sentiment. No positioning strategy on record.

Deal is 11 days old with one intro call (no recording) and zero follow-up engagement. Rep promised Showpad link and discovery scheduling on ~21 Jun but has not actioned it. With 264 days to close, there is runway, but a deal that goes dark from day one signals low buyer urgency or that we are not yet relevant to their timeline.

No pain, no metrics, no decision criteria, no competitive intelligence, no Economic Buyer mapped. Every MEDDPICC element is a gap. The discovery call (promised but not scheduled) is the gate to everything; without it, we cannot build a defensible case, position against JAGGAER, or multi-thread into power roles.

RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder — This deal is 11 days old and has been silent since the intro call on 15 Jun 2026 (no recording). The rep promised Showpad demo videos and discovery scheduling on ~21 Jun but has not actioned it. With 264 days to close, there is ample runway, but a deal that goes dark from day one signals low buyer urgency or that we are not yet relevant. The rank-1 move is for the deal owner (Justin Ajmo) to re-engage Joe Schell within 48 hours: deliver the Showpad link, acknowledge the delay, and lock the discovery call for next week (by 3 Jul). If Schell does not respond within 5 business days, escalate to the CFO (the deal owner's manager may need to intro) to assess whether this is a real opportunity or a courtesy exploration.

SINGLE-THREADED AND NO CHAMPION — Joe Schell (Assistant VP, Procurement) is the only buyer-side contact role, and with no engagement since the intro call 11 days ago, he is showing no champion behavior (no multi-threading, no urgency, no advocacy). At a $125k deal with a March 2027 close, we need at least 3-4 power contacts (CFO, CIO/IT Ops, COO) and a champion who is actively driving the evaluation internally. The discovery call is the gate to ask Schell to introduce us to the CFO (Nathan Bull) and CIO (Maurice Raghubar) so we can map the decision process and budget authority. If Schell is unwilling to multi-thread us, he is not a champion — he is a polite responder — and we need to find another entry point or disqualify.

DISCOVERY CALL the relevant stakeholder TO EVERYTHING — With no recorded intro call and no discovery yet run, we have zero MEDDPICC depth: no pain, no metrics, no Economic Buyer, no decision criteria, no process, no competitive intelligence beyond one name. Every move (champion-building, competitive positioning, multi-threading, pain quantification, ROI workshop, demo) is blocked until we run discovery. The discovery call (promised but not scheduled) is the single highest-impact activity in the next 7 days. Prep a tight discovery agenda: quantified pain (what is broken, what does it cost), decision criteria (what are you evaluating, what matters most), competitive landscape (who else are you talking to, what is your current process), budget authority (who approves the spend), and timeline (what are the gates between now and March 2027). Do not leave discovery without multi-threading into at least one power role (CFO or CIO).
